title: Budget-capped funded proposer variant (batch candidate generation)
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. A funded-lane variant of the proposer that batch-generates candidates under a hard per-task budget cap. Out of v0.1.0 scope and kept lane:donated here because PLAN.md defines no escrow/budget cap yet; when scoped the lane flips to funded with fundedBudgetUsd set and a hard cap enforced that never exceeds escrow.
objective: Design/build the funded-lane proposer variant that batch-generates candidates under a hard per-task budget cap, still feeding the review queue.
acceptanceCriteria: Batch-generates (oer, standard) candidates under a hard per-task budget cap (fundedBudgetUsd) and never exceeds the task's escrow.
Still feeds the review queue with no auto-publish; candidates require educator review.
Out of v0.1.0 scope: representative task; when scoped, lane flips to funded with fundedBudgetUsd set.
Code MIT; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; the commit is DCO signed-off; no credentials or secrets committed.
